stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 25 patients. The sample mean is 280.8 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 103.4 mmHg. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 258.5 mmHg and standard deviation of 77.3 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 280.8, 95% CI = [238.1, 323.5]. Without outliers: mean = 258.5, 95% CI = [226.3, 290.7]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [226.3, 323.5]. Point estimate: 269.6 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [226.3, 323.5].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
